entry.title: 邻家被盗
entry.content:
Mrs. Liu, a Mandarin-speaking woman is telephoning Crime Stoppers to report a crime currently being committed outside her residence.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Thank you for calling Crime Stoppers. How can I help you?11
M1我要报案，有人正在入室行窃。我从我卧室的窗户能看到三个人，他们闯进了我邻居的家里。I’d  like  to  report  a  crime,  a  burglary.  I  can  see  three  people  out  of  my bedroom window. They’ve broken into my neighbour’s house.
E2OK. Stay where  you  are  and don’t let them see  you looking at them. Can you please tell me your address so I can send out a police car immediately?31
M2是 Strathfield 区皇后大街 56 号。三个男人正在把液晶电视、音响，还有台式机电脑往一辆面包车里搬呢。奇怪，报警器怎么没响？No.  56,  Queens  Street,  Strathfield.  Three  men  are  now  carrying  the  LCD TV,  hi-fi  system  and  desktop  computer  into  a  van.  It’s  very  strange.  How come the alarm wasn’t set off?
E3Could you describe their appearance to me as well as the van – and can you see the license plate? Any details you provide will be of great assistance to the police.31
M3他们身穿黑色 T 恤衫，运动裤。是一辆老款的白色丰田车。天色太暗了，我看不清楚车牌。警察怎么还没来？They’re wearing black T-shirts and sports shorts. It’s an old model Toyota. I can’t see the plate clearly because it’s too dark outside. How come the police are not here yet?
E4I’ve notified the police already and they should arrive there in the next five minutes or so. Do you know  your neighbour’s name, where  they are right now and when they will come back?34
M4她叫 Alice  Green。她现在在马来西亚度假。她请我在她不在家的时候帮她收邮件和喂猫。她要过两个星期才回来。Her name is Alice Green and she is on holiday in Malaysia. She asked me to pick up her mail and feed her cat while she’s away. She’ll be back in two weeks.
E5Thank you. Now, you may want to remain anonymous, but if you’re willing to  give  us  your  name  or  talk  to  the  field  officer  later,  it  may  be  of  great assistance to the police.34
M5我不介意。我叫刘晓红，如果警察有什么问题，我很乐意和他们聊聊。我这辈子还从来就没遇到入室行窃的事儿。I don’t mind. My name is Liu Xiaohong and I’m happy to talk to the officers if  they  have  any  more  questions.  I’ve  never  come  across  a  burglary  my whole life.
E6If you worry about your safety, please call your local Neighbourhood Watch Centre.  I’m sure they can help  you  with how to  protect  yourself  and  your home.27
M6话是这么说，但我还是小心一点好。糟糕，他们正要开车跑了！That being said, I’d better be alert. Oh no, now they are driving away!

entry.title: 缉毒伸冤
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Cheng (C), a Mandarin speaking man, and Mr. Smith (S), a lawyer regarding some legal issues.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，史密斯先生。今天为我是来寻求你的帮助的。我听说，你是本市有名的刑事案件方面的律师，对吗？Hello Mr. Smith. I am here today to seek your help. I heard that you are a famous criminal lawyer in this city. Is that correct?
M1Mr. Cheng, I specialise in criminal law with more than 25 years of experience in various criminal cases ranging from burglary to murder. Please give me a brief description about your situation.32
E2两天前，几个警察突然带着搜查令进入我居住的房子，他们从我家搜出了一包毒品，但我根本不知道那是哪来的。Two days ago, several police officers suddenly came to my house with a search warrant and they seized a packet of drugs from my house but I had no idea how it got there.
M2I am familiar with similar matters. The key question is if you are absolutely certain you were not in possession of any drugs. Do you suspect someone may have planted the drugs at your house?35
E3我很确定。关于你的第二个问题，那可能是跟我住一起的任何一个人。但不管是谁，这个人已经严重的败坏了我的名誉，使我的未来岌岌可危。I am positive. As to your second question, it could have been anyone living with   me.   But   whoever   did   this   has   seriously   harmed   my   personal reputation and put my future at stake.
M3Your concern is understandable, although not completely necessary, Mr. Cheng. Now I have another question for you, are you currently on bail? If so, when did you receive it?29
E4昨天，地方法院的法官准予我获得保释，但前提是，我必须上交我的护照。这个案子会在 12 月 23 号开庭审理。 I was granted bail by a magistrate in the Local Court yesterday on the condition that I had to surrender my passport. The matter is due to be heard on the twenty-third of December.I  was  granted  bail  by  a  magistrate  in  the  Local  Court  yesterday  on  the condition  that  I  had  to  surrender  my  passport.  The  matter  is  due  to  be heard on the twenty-third of December.
M4Are you aware of the exact criminal charge laid against you? If you are, we should then lose no time in consulting the relevant legislation and case law.28
E5警察说，我被指控的罪名是私藏毒品。好像还有别的什么，我不大清楚。The  police  told  me  that  I  have  been  charged  with  illegal  possession  of drugs. There could be something else….I’m not too sure.
M5Have the police told you the amount of drugs that were found in your house? You need to inform me of the exact quantity, as it will make a big difference to your defense.34
E6我不大记得了，但可能是超过 100 克的。为什么问这个呢？你是在怀疑我的清白吗？I cannot remember but it couldn’t be more than 100 grams. Why do you ask this? Do you doubt my innocence?
M6On the contrary, I always have confidence in my clients. We’ll sign a costs agreement and I’ll start working on your case immediately.23

entry.title: 租赁纠纷
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Chen (C), a Mandarin speaking man, and Steven (S), a solicitor in the local community legal centre. Mr. Chen is seeking advice on problems between him and his landlord.
IDOriginalTranslated
M1你好。我想问一下 Steven 先生在不在？我跟他约了今天十点钟的会面。 Hello, I’d like to know whether Steven is here or not. I’ve made an appointment to see him at 10 o’clock today.Hello, I’d like to know whether Steven is here or not. I’ve made an appointment to see him at 10 o’clock today.
E1Are you Mr. Chen? I think you are looking for me as I’m the only Steven here. Please take a seat. How may I help you today?27
M2是这样，我的朋友建议我到这里来咨询一下律师，因为我在准备起诉我的房东，来捍卫自己的合理权益。因为我房东他过分了！ Look, my friend suggested that I come here and speak to some lawyers because I’m planning to sue my landlord and fight for my rights. He’s ridiculous!Look, my friend suggested that I come here and speak to some lawyers because I’m planning to sue my landlord and fight for my rights. He’s ridiculous!
E2It seems that you’ve got a problem or dispute with your landlord. But I can reassure you that you’re protected by the “Office for Fair Trading” as a tenant in New South Wales. Tell me, what’s the problem?38
M3两个星期之前，我跟我的房东说我想搬走。从那以后，他每天都带不同的人来我住的地方看房。我跟他表达过我的不满，但是他都不理不睬。I told my landlord two weeks ago that I wanted to move out. Since then, he’s taken different people into the place for an open house every day.  I made a complaint to him but he just ignored me.
E3You’re absolutely right in the sense that he can’t come whenever he likes. Landlords are only allowed to enter the premises at certain times for certain reasons and in most cases have to give you notice first.37
M4我也这样认为。当我问他怎么能这样做的时候，他只说他这是在保护他自己的权益。他想要尽快把这间房租出去。That’s what I thought. When I asked him why he was doing that, he just told me that he’s protecting his rights by doing so.  He wants to rent the place out as soon as possible.
E4If this matter cannot be resolved between you and your landlord, I’d suggest you take this to the Consumer, Trader and Tenancy Tribunal, which specialises in handling tenancy disputes.30
M5上法庭吗？那我需要请律师吗？会花很多钱吧？还有啊，我一点英语都不会讲，我跟法官沟通不了，可怎么办呢？Going to court? Do I need to hire a lawyer? Does it cost a lot? On top of that,  I  don’t  speak  English  at  all,  what  if  I  can’t  communicate  with  the judge?
E5You can represent yourself at the tribunal. The tribunal will provide interpreters to assist people whose first language is not English. As for the application fee, it’s $38 for tenancy disputes.33
M6那好，我明白了。我想问一下我可以去哪里拿一份申请表呢？今天真是麻烦你了。OK,  I  understand.  I’m  just  wondering  where  I  can  get  a  copy  of  the application form. Thank you very much for today.
E6I can give you an application form, just a second.10

entry.title: 百日咳症
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mrs. Li and her GP, Dr. Smith, regarding her daughter’s whooping cough.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mrs. Li. How can I help you today?10
M1医生你好,我今天带了我的大女儿来看你。她最近一直咳嗽，不能停的那种，而且鼻子不通气，打喷嚏还有点发低烧。我的女儿到底是怎么了？Hello,  doctor.  I’ve  brought  my  oldest  daughter  here  today.  She  has  been coughing and couldn’t stop. She also has a stuffy nose, sneezing and mild fever. What’s wrong with her?
E2Well, based on what you have just described, I am afraid that your daughter may be in the early stages of whopping cough. The symptoms could develop further if it is not treated.33
M2真的？这听上去太可怕了！那这种病是不是很严重呢？我的女儿是怎么患上这种病的呢？最近她们学校感冒的人特别多，和这个有没有关系呢？Really? That sounds horrible! Is it a serious disease?   Where did she get it? Currently, so many students in her school have caught colds, could that be the cause?
E3Whooping cough is a serious respiratory infection that causes a long coughing illness. In babies, it can lead to pneumonia and brain damage and it can be life- threatening. Older children’s lives are not at risk, but they can spread this illness to others, including babies.45
M3我家里还有一个小宝贝儿子呢，是不是就是说我的小宝宝现在也被传染上百日咳了呢？ 但是我的小宝宝没有咳嗽的症状，所以我今天就没有带他来。I have another little baby boy at home. Do you mean my little baby has also contracted  whooping  cough?  But  he  has  no  symptom  of  coughing  so  I  didn’t bring him here today.
E4Yes, it’s possible! Whooping cough is highly infectious in the first 3 weeks; it spreads easily through families, childcare centres and schools so it is likely that your baby boy has already been infected. Some newborn babies do not cough at all. Instead, they turn blue. So it is important that you bring your baby boy here for a check up.61
M4百日咳是怎样传染的呢？那我怎样防止我的孩子患上百日咳呢？ 我的小儿子在 2 个月，4 个月和 6 个月的时候打过疫苗，这一点用也没有吗?How  does  whooping  cough  spread?  What  do  I  need  to  do  to  prevent  my children from getting the disease? My youngest son was immunised against it when he was 2 months, 4 months and 6 months old. Was that helpful at all?
E5Whooping cough is spread when the infected person coughs and the bacteria coughed into the air is inhaled by babies, children or adults nearby. These people are in danger of getting whooping cough. Just keep in mind that even immunised children can sometimes catch whooping cough.46
M5那我知道了，但是你还没有告诉我我都需要做什么去保护我的孩子。 有什么药物治疗的方法吗?Oh,  I  see.  But  you  still  haven’t  told  me  what  I  need  to  do  to  protect  my children. Is there any medicine that can treat the disease?
E6If whooping cough is detected early enough, like in your daughter’s case, a course of antibiotics could be prescribed, which I am going to do in a second. After 5 days of treatment, enough bacteria will be killed to stop it spreading to others. The best way to protect your children would be keeping them away from the infected.59
M6我知道了，医生，谢谢。我明天就带小宝宝来见你。I  understand.  Thank  you,  doctor.  I  will  bring  my  little  baby  here  to  see  you tomorrow.

entry.title: 初诊肥胖
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a doctor and a patient who has just been diagnosed with diabetes.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, thank you for coming in today. As I explained to you last time, you have been diagnosed with diabetes; Your body can’t produce enough insulin to break down the sugar in your body, so the sugar remains in your blood.
M1但是我还是不明白，我怎么会有糖尿病哪？ 我不喜欢吃甜食，怎么会有那么多的糖在我体内哪？ 还有为什么我最近会觉得有些虚弱，疲倦，而且有时还会头晕？42
E2Well, how much sugar remains in your body has little to do with how much sweet food you eat. Basically, without enough insulin, your body can’t break down the sugars in food into a form it can absorb, so the cells are not getting enough energy, which is why you get tired.But I still don’t understand. How come I have diabetes? I don’t like sweet food. Why is there so much sugar stored in my body? Also, why do I often feel weak, tired and sometimes dizzy?
M2我明白了，那有什么方法可以完全治愈吗？有什么药物还是我必须要打针呢？ 我有一些朋友也有糖尿病，他们每天吃饭之前都要打针，真是太可怕了！52
E3You have been diagnosed with diabetes type 2. There is no cure for this type of diabetes at the moment. However, you can control it with medication and you will also have to follow a strict healthy diet. If you are careful enough with your diet and you also do exercise you shouldn’t need to have any injections.I see. So is there any way to cure it? Is there any medicine for the disease or do I need the injection? Some of my friends have diabetes too and they have to take a shot before meals every day. It’s horrible!
M3那我就放心了，那么在饮食上我都需要注意些什么呢？是不是一点甜的都不能吃啊？55
E4You need to eat food that has sugar but does not taste sweet at all, and which releases sugar into your body very slowly. And the “sugar” we are talking about here is not the sugar you use in cooking or put into your coffee.I’m relieved to hear that. What do I need to pay attention to regarding my diet? I can’t eat anything sweet, right?
M4那我怎么样才能知道我的血糖指数呢？ 每次都需要来医院检查吗？45
E5Not really, you can check it with a monitor that you can carry around. It is very easy to use.So how can I find out what my blood sugar level is? Do I need to come to the hospital to check it every time?
M5我最近总是感觉很累，所以基本上已经不怎么锻炼了，但是我以前经常打兵乓球和羽毛球，您说我是不是应该继续呢?20
E6Yes, of course. These exercises will keep you fit and help you control your diabetes. Just make sure you eat enough food before doing exercise so that your sugar levels wouldn’t get too low.I always feel very tired lately, so basically I don’t do much exercise. But I used to play ping pong and badminton very often. Do you think I should take them up again?

entry.title: 甲亢就医
entry.content:
The following is a dialogue between a doctor and a patient with hyperthyroidism.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon Mrs. Wang. What seems to be the problem?10
M1医生，最近我容易发火并且心慌。以前没有过这样的感觉，只是近两个月才有的事。不知道是什么引起的。Doctor, recently I’ve been losing my temper easily and have palpitations. I’ve never had that kind of feeling before. It just started happening in the last two months. I don’t know why.
E2Have you lost any weight recently? Have you been sweating as much as you usually do? Have you been sleeping okay?21
M2尽管我饭量增加 ，两个月来，已经瘦了四公斤了。我比以前出汗出得多了，简直无法忍受热的天气。有的时候晚上会失眠，但不是每天都这样。I eat more than usual but I’ve still lost 4 kilograms in the last two months. I sweat more than before as well, and I can’t stand hot weather. Sometimes I have difficulties falling asleep at night but not every day.
E3Let me examine you. Swallow please. There are some tests you will have to get done and we will see you in one week. At that time we will start on some more definite therapy for your condition.36
M3谢谢您，医生。请您告诉我我得了什么病？但愿不是很严重。Thank you, doctor. Please tell me what kind of disease I have. I hope it is nothing serious.
E4I suspect that you might have hyperthyroidism. We need to arrange a time for you to have some thyroid function tests done.22
M4医生，最坏的结果会是什么？甲状腺出问题可以采用药物治疗吗？我不想做手术。So doctor, what could be the worst scenario? Can thyroid conditions be cured by medicine? I don’t want to have an operation
E5I can’t make a diagnosis before getting the test results. But of course, medication such as anti-thyroid drugs is an option. The major disadvantages of medicinal treatment are the long treatment time and the high chance that the disease will reoccur.42
M5我明白了。那药物治疗是不是还有什么副作用？ 那如果必须做手术，会是什么样的手术呢？肯定会在颈部留下伤疤的吧？I see. Does the drug therapy also cause some side effects? If there has to be surgery, what kind of surgery will I need to have? It will definitely leave a scar on my neck, right?
E6Yes, that’s why we don’t suggest drug therapy. As for surgery, I’m afraid it will leave a scar on your neck. The worst scenario for your condition is a subtotal thyroidectomy. But Mrs Wang, don’t worry at this stage. We will talk about all the options that are open to you after we get the results.57
M6谢谢您，医生。我会先去安排检查的时间，希望没什么大碍。等我拿到结果再来找您。Thanks, doctor. I will go schedule a test first. I hope it’s nothing serious. I’ll come back to you as soon as the results come out.

entry.title: 眼睛测试
entry.content:
Mr. Wang is having an appointment with Dr. Jackman, an ophthalmologist, at the public hospital for an eye test.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning. Mr. Wang, isn’t it? I am Dr. Jackman. Follow me please, and I will  check  your  vision.  Is  this  the  first  time  you  have  visited  our ophthalmology department?30
M1是的，我是第一次来这里。最近我看东西越来越模糊了，看一会儿眼睛就很酸，而且经常流眼泪。Yes. It’s my first time here. My vision has become more and more blurred lately. My eyes get very sore after looking at anything for a while and I have been getting watery eyes.
E2Okay, let’s take a look. Do you wear glasses for reading, driving, or watching TV or have you ever worn contacts?21
M2我没有隐形眼镜。平时一般不戴眼镜，只有看书的时候才戴，看电视也不用戴。医生，我是不是得了什么很严重的眼病呀，是白内障吗？I don’t have contact lenses and I usually don’t need to wear glasses unless I’m reading. I don’t need them for watching TV. Doctor, do I have a serious eye condition? Is it cataracts?
E3We won’t know until we’ve done all the tests. Now, please put on your glasses and look at the eye chart. If you can’t see it clearly, just blink and look at it again.34
M3我看头两排没有什么问题，但是第三排就比较费劲了，更别说第四排和最后一排了。There’s no problem for me to see the top two lines but the third line is a bit difficult for me, let alone the fourth and last lines.
E4OK. Please let me have your glasses. I need to measure the two lenses and then I’ll return them to you. If you can wait outside – you’ll be called when I’ve finished.34
M4大概要等多久？我还要回去接孩子，买菜做饭呢！还需要做什么别的测试吗？How long do I have to wait? I need to pick up my child, shop for groceries and prepare dinner. Do I need to take any further tests?
E5It won’t be long as all the work will be done with the assistance of a computer. After that, we’ll complete the optometry tests with you and check your eye pressure.31
M5眼压测试？听起来有点吓人。你能告诉我这个测试具体是怎么做的呢？会很疼吗？Eye pressure test? Sounds a bit scary. Could you please tell me in a bit more detail how the test is done? Will it be very painful?
E6You don’t need to be scared. It’s a very easy and safe eye procedure. A device blows air into your eye and you just keep your eye as open as possible.31
M6那好吧。我会尽力配合的。谢谢你医生。All right. I’ll try my best to do as you’ve said. Thank you, doctor.

entry.title: 儿科例检
entry.content:
Mrs. Lin, a Mandarin-speaking woman, has brought her three-month old son to see Mr. Allen White, a pediatrician, for a routine check-up.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1早上好，医生。我今天把我的小麦克带来了。请你帮着检查一下。Good morning, doctor. I have brought my little Mike. I would like you to have a look at him.
M1Good morning, Mrs. Lin. Nice to see you. Let me see. What a chubby little boy! First of all, I need to check his weight and length. If you could put him down and undress him that would be great.40
E2好的。这是宝宝的健康记录本。我已经做过产后检查了。宝宝上一次体检的时候，那个助产士说一切正常，没什么值得担心的大问题。All right. Here is his health check record. I have done the postnatal check and the midwife said he was perfect and there was nothing in particular to worry about at his last examination.
M2I can see from his blue book that he’s got all the immunisations from the GP. That’s excellent to start with. Are you giving him any formula or vitamins at all?
E3孩子一直都是喝我的奶。别的什么也没吃过。但是，最近我发现他的体重长得慢了。我们有点着急。I’ve been breastfeeding him all the time and we haven’t fed him anything else. But, I’ve found that he’s been slow at gaining weight lately. We are getting a bit worried.
M3At three months of age, babies normally go through a growth spurt. That means they are growing muscles and reducing fat and stop gaining weight for about three weeks.29
E4另外，他现在睡觉也不如以前好了，总是要哭闹好一会儿，而且要我们抱着才能入睡。这正常吗？Also, his sleep is not as good as before. He will cry for quite a while and can only fall asleep in our arms. Is this normal?
M4Babies also have a sleep cycle change at this age. Now they only sleep three times during the day for no more than two hours and much less at night.30
E5我看不少国内的网站说，这个岁数的宝宝应该开始喂辅食了。你觉得现在用得着吗？该吃点什么好呢？I checked some websites in China and they all say a baby of his age should be fed supplementary foods. Do you think it’s necessary and what should we give him?
M5No, it’s too early for you to do so. It was a practice years ago but now we’ve learned that babies don’t need to be fed with solid food until around six months.33
E6看来中国和澳洲抚养孩子的方法还真的很不一样。那下一次检查时什么时候？我现在能预约吗？It seems that there is a whole lot of difference between Australia and China when it comes to raising a baby. So when will be the next check-up? Can I book an appointment now?
M6I can book you in for Thursday, the 5th of July, which is in another 3 months. But you need to call one week before to confirm.27

entry.title: 入院手续
entry.content:
The following is a dialogue between a nurse and a patient who’s just been admitted to hospital. The nurse is taking the patient’s personal details and asking about the patient’s general state of health.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Jason, my name is Jessica. I’m a registered nurse and I’ll be looking after you today. How are you feeling at the moment?23
M1早上好。我现在感觉还可以。谢谢关心。因为之前没有在澳大利亚住过院，所以感觉有一点紧张。Good morning. I am feeling fine now. Thank you for asking. But I’m a bit nervous as it’s my first time to be hospitalised in Australia.
E2Don’t worry. There’s nothing for you to be concerned about. Now, I’ve got a few questions to ask you and some paperwork to go through for your admission. Shall we start now?31
M2是的。当然！你有什么问题尽管问，还好你们今天请了口译员来，不然我会很担心如何沟通的。Yes,Ofcourse!Pleasegoahead.Iwouldhavebeensoworriedabout communicating with you if you hadn’t hired an interpreter for me today.
E3No worries. Firstly, I’d like to take your personal details. Your surname is Lee, and your given name is Yang, am I correct? Could you tell me your marital status as well?32
M3是对的。不过我可以看一下你怎么拼写我的名字吗？想确认一下。哦，谢谢。是对的。我结婚了，但是太太现在在国内。Yes, that’s right. But can I have a look at how you spelled my name? I just want to make sure. Thanks. That’s correct. I am married, but my wife is in China at the moment.
E4Are you still living at the same address, Unit 16, 38 Adelaide St, Thomas Town? Could you tell me your date of birth and your next of kin in Australia as well as your religion, if you have one?39
M4好的。我还住在这个地址。我出生于 1958 年 11 月 13 号。我和我女儿住一起，我信佛教。OK. I am still living at that address. I was born on the 13th of November, 1958. I live here with my daughter and I am Buddhist.
E5Can you give me a few details about your medical history? Have you or any close relatives ever had hypertension, diabetes, asthma, heart disease, or cancer? Have you had any operations?31
M5我七年前得了高血压，两年前确诊得了糖尿病。我用严格的饮食和锻炼来控制。5 年前得了心脏病我做了一次手术。I was diagnosed with hypertension 7 years ago and diabetes 2 years ago. I am managing these conditions with a strict diet and regular exercises. I had heart surgery 5 years ago.
E6Are you taking any medication? Are you allergic to any drugs or food? Do you smoke or drink?18
M6我只是在吃一些维生素。到目前为止，我没有任何过敏的药物。我 5 年前心脏手术过后就不吸烟了，只是偶尔喝一点红酒。I’m only taking some vitamins and I am not allergic to any medication. I quit smoking after I had the heart surgery 5 years ago but I do drink some.

entry.title: 反复补牙
entry.content:
A woman is visiting a dentist.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1医生您好，我的牙龈总是出血，口腔还发出一种臭味，这种情况已经差不多一年了。 Hello. My gums are always bleeding and I have bad breath. It has been like this for almost a year.Hello. My gums are always bleeding and I have bad breath. It has been like this for almost a year.
M1Does the bleeding occur by itself or is it induced by some irritation? That is to say, do your gums bleed when you brush your teeth or when you eat or do they bleed for no apparent reason at all?40
E2不知道，我只知道牙龈流血。还有，我后面的牙有个洞，我补的牙里面的填充物掉出来了。所以牙又开始疼了。 (I don’t know, I just know that my gums have been bleeding. Also, one of my back teeth has a cavity. The filling in it fell out so I have a toothache again.I don’t know, I just know that my gums have been bleeding. Also, one of my back teeth has a cavity. The filling in it fell out so I have a toothache again.
M2Let me have a look. Please sit in the chair. Lean your head back against the chair. It is a deep cavity. We’ll have an x-ray taken of that tooth just to make sure about the apex. If it is in good condition, I will treat it.48
E3这颗牙还可以再补吗？如果不能补, 我可真的没辙了。近来每天晚上，我都疼得睡不好觉。 (Can it be filled again? I don’t know what to do if it can’t be filled. I haven’t been sleeping well due to the pain.Can it be filled again? I don’t know what to do if it can’t be filled. I haven’t been sleeping well due to the pain.
M3Yes, it can be filled. I’ll clean it now. Please let me know if it hurts. Bite please. Now please open your mouth again. Do you feel anything abnormal when you clench your teeth?34
E4感觉不舒服，而且我吃冷的或热的东西，牙就疼得更厉害了。晚上的时候疼痛也会加重。即使我有时候吃止痛药，也没多大作用。I’m feeling uncomfortable and the pain gets worse when I eat cold or hot things. And it’s more serious during the night. Even though I sometimes take painkillers, it doesn’t help much.
M4From my observations, I’d say you have inflammation of the gums as well as pulpitis. We’ll have an x-ray done as soon as possible to see whether there are any wisdom teeth that need to be extracted.35
E5我之前没有做过牙齿的 X 光检查，会疼吗？另外，这个费用能用政府的保健医疗卡报销吗？I’ve never had a dental x-ray before. Does it hurt? By the way, is it covered by Medicare?
M5I’m afraid not. If you have private health insurance, you may be entitled to claim some of the money back. I am not an expert though, you probably need to consult your private health insurance provider.36
E6我没有私人保险，不过现在申请可定也来不及了，看来这次看牙将会是一笔很大的支出了。不管怎么说，谢谢您医生。I don’t have any private health insurance but it is now too late to apply. It seems that this time this dental problem will cost me a lot. But anyway, thank you, doctor.
M6You’re welcome. I’ll get my secretary to make another appointment for you.12

entry.title: 背颈劳损
entry.content:
The following dialogue takes place between a doctor and a Mandarin speaking patient, regarding a work related injury.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Mrs Li, I’ll start by asking some questions about your background, especially about your work history. I hope you don’t mind. What’s the highest education you obtained in China and what sorts of jobs did you have there.38
M1我高中毕业之后考上了一所大专技校，相当于这边的 TAFE，我当时学的专业是机械。所以毕业之后就在一个机床零件加工厂工作。I went to a technical junior college after I finished high school. It’s similar to TAFE in Australia. I was studying mechanics so I began to work at a machine parts processing and manufacturing plant after graduating.
E2Can you be more specific? What were your responsibilities in the factory? For example, was that assembly work or a sales role?22
M2是个零件加工的工作，我也做过销售，但那时后来的事了，大部分的工作都是做流水线。It was for parts processing. I also worked as a salesperson but that was some time afterwards. Most of my work was done on the assembly lines.
E3So after you came to Australia, what jobs did you have? I can see from your file that you started working in a factory called Star Technology in 1999.29
M3没错，在那之前只做过一些零零散散的工作，时间太久了，也记不太清楚具体做过什么了。是的，我 99 年进了那个工厂之后就一直在那工作，直到后来落下脖子疼和背疼的毛病，才不得不辞职。Yes. I only had some casual jobs before working there. But I can’t remember the details as it was a long time ago. Yes, I was working in that factory since I started in 1999, until I noticed these neck and back problems caused by this job. So I quit.
E4I see. Since you mentioned neck and back pain, we can come to this first. Was there a particular accident that caused your neck and back injury?27
M4没有出什么事故，但是我长年累月的做一个工作，坐着工作的时间太长，而且经常需要搬很沉的箱子，才落下了这个病。No, there were no accidents. But I have such problems because I had to keep doing the same work for so many years and I had been sitting for so long. Also, I often had to move heavy boxes.
E5So you were saying that the pain was there a while ago and it has worsened over time? When did you first go the see your GP about this matter?30
M5大概两年前吧，当时不知道是不是工伤，但我的家庭医生认为这和我的工作有很大的关系。About two years ago. I wasn’t sure whether it was a work related injury, but my family doctor suggested it was closely related to my work.
E6I will ask some detailed questions later about your previous job and about your worker’s compensation claim.17
M6没问题，我有一些书面材料，马上拿给你看。Sure. I have some paperwork that I would like to show you right away.

entry.title: 儿童接种
entry.content:
This dialogue is between a parent and an officer from the Australian Childhood Immunisation Register.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon, I’m Lion Hutton. You said you have some questions regarding the immunisations that your child received. What can I do for you today?25
M1我想我儿子已经接受了所有该做的免疫。但是上个星期五我收到这封信说还有其他种类的免疫。我感到有点诧异。I think my son has received all the necessary vaccination shots. But I was surprised when I received this letter last Friday, saying there are other kinds of vaccinations needed.
E2How old is your son? Do you have his immunisation record book with you now? Also, can I have a look at the letter you mentioned?26
M2我儿子上个星期刚满 17 个月。是的，我今天把免疫记录本带来了，还有这封信，给您。last week. Yes, I have brought his immunisation record with me today and this is the letter. Here you are.
E3Let me see. Yes, actually this letter is from the Australian Childhood Immunisation Register. It automatically sends out reminders to parents when their children are due for immunisation.28
M3但是我儿子已经做过好几种疫苗接种了。我记得第一次是在他出生的时候，以后又接种过两次。所以我认为他不需要再接受免疫了。But my son has already received several vaccines. I remember the first shot was given right after he was born and there were two times after that. So I don’t think he needs any more vaccinations.
E4You were perfectly right in saying that he has received several immunisations. The fact though is that certain types have to be given more than once to provide the child with sufficient immunity.33
M4好吧，就算某些疫苗需要接种一次以上，但需要三次吗？小孩子能受得了吗？你不认为这封信寄给我是寄错了吗？Alright, even if some vaccinations have to be given more than once, does it have to be three times? Don’t you think it’s too much for a young kid to cope with? Don’t you think the letter was sent to me by mistake?
E5We sometimes send 5 reminders. In most cases, it’s recommended that immunisations should be repeated several times. In the case of diphtheria, tetanus and whooping cough, the injection is given 5 times over 5 years.35
M5但是我小的时候都没有接受过这么多次免疫，可你看，我身体这么好，连一般的感冒都不得。But I didn’t receive so many vaccinations when I was a kid. But you can see I am so physically fit and healthy that I rarely catch a cold.
E6In the past, the vaccine wasn’t as available as it is now. Besides there were many deaths and illnesses in the past that could have actually been due to a lack of immunisation.33
M6我想你是对的。过去的医生没有合适的办法和仪器来正确诊断疾病。I think you are right as doctors in the past didn’t have proper methods and equipment to give accurate diagnoses.

entry.title: 女儿水痘
entry.content:
This dialogue is between a woman, Mrs. Wang, and her GP, regarding some rashes on her daughter’s skin.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon Mrs. Wang. This must be your daughter Lucy. How can I help you?15
M1我女儿发烧 37.8 度有两天了。她身上和脸上都长了很多小红点儿。但她的食欲很好，睡眠也没问题。您快帮她看看吧。我很担心。My daughter has had a temperature of 37.8 degrees for two days now. There are many red spots over her body and face. But she still has a good appetite and has no sleeping problems. Please have a look at her. I am so worried.
E2When did you first notice the rashes? Does she feel itchy or have any pain? I would like to find out some more details.24
M2昨天她从幼儿园回来说她不舒服。当时我看到她胸前有一些粉红色的皮疹。今天早上看到她脸上也起了很多疹子和水泡似的东西。我女儿觉得很痒，昨天晚上都影响她睡觉了。She complained she was not feeling well after coming back from kindergarten yesterday. I saw some pink rashes on her chest and I found many rashes and something like blisters on her face this morning. She said they were so itchy that she didn’t sleep well last night.
E3Let me have a look at Lucy. The rash appears in crops and papules, and there are blisters on the body, extremities and face, looking like ’dewdrops’ on a red base. She has chickenpox, which is an infectious disease.38
M3水痘？估计是幼儿园有其他的孩子得了水痘，我的女儿被传染了。那会不会留下疤痕啊? 要多久能好？我应该怎么在家照顾她啊？Chickenpox? It seems that my daughter contracted chickenpox from other kids who have it in the kindergarten. Will it leave any scars? How long does it take for the disease to be cured? What do I need to do at home to take care of her?
E4The crusts fall off in one to three weeks, leaving no scars. Please keep her away from other children until the crusts fall off completely. Give her plenty of water to drink. Keep her skin clean in order to prevent infection. Don’t let her scratch her skin.47
M4好的，我会的。不过要是特别痒她肯定会不停的抓的。您能给她开些口服药或者药膏吗？我不想我的女儿那么难受。OK. I will. But she will keep scratching if it is too itchy. Could you please prescribe some oral medicine or ointment for her? I don’t want to see her suffering.
E5I will give her some lotion and some medicine to control the itching. Lucy should start feeling better after about a week.21
M5好的，谢谢医生。我女儿昨天还和一个小朋友一起玩儿，希望她的朋友不会有什么事。OK. Thank you, doctor. My daughter played with one of her friends yesterday. I hope she won’t get infected.
E6If the fever persists or the symptoms get worse after a few days, please bring her back to see me again.21
M6我知道了。但愿我不用再过来见您。再见。I understand. I hope this is the last time that I see you for this matter. Good bye.

entry.title: 楼梯摔伤
entry.content:
The following dialogue takes place between a specialist and a patient, Mrs. Li, who has just broken her hip and arm.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mrs. Li. I’m Doctor Clark, an orthopaedic surgeon. How can I help you today?16
M1我从楼梯上摔下来，当时顿时觉得左胯部疼得厉害，根本没法自己站起来。现在还是觉得很疼。I felt a sharp pain in my left hip after I fell down the stairs. I couldn’t stand up by myself. Now it still hurts.
E2When did it happen? Have you taken any painkillers? If not, how did you manage the pain?17
M2昨天下午发生的。我什么药都没吃，我的家庭医生把我转介到这里的。主要是伤到了我的左胯部还有左臂。It happened yesterday afternoon. I didn’t take any medicine. My family doctor referred me to you. My left hip and left arm were seriously injured.
E3The x-ray and MRI results show a fracture in your left arm. To make sure the bone heals properly, I suggest you be admitted to the hospital. You need an operation to fix it. Is your left elbow still in pain?41
M3是的。受伤后左胳膊僵硬，发紫发胀。有时候还觉得很麻。什么都做不了,现在只能先在家。而且就算什么都不做，伤处都会疼。Yes. I felt stiffness in my left arm and it was swollen and turned blue. Sometimes I feel numbness. I’m not able to do anything now and have to stay at home for the time being. The injured parts are still in pain even when I stay still.
E4We will give you an “L” type plaster cast on your elbow and shoulder. You will have to have it for about six weeks.24
M4除了带石膏托外，我还需要注意什么吗？这是我第一次受这么严重的伤。Apart from wearing the plaster cast, what else do I need to pay attention to? It’s the first time that I’ve gotten injured so badly.
E5When you rest in bed, please keep your elbow higher than your heart. Move your finger joints from time to time. Keep an eye on the colour of your fingers. If you have pain and numbness or if the colour of the fingers turns paler or darker, please come back to see me straight away.55
M5好的，我知道一旦伤到骨头就很麻烦，需要很长时间才能恢复。我还想麻烦您一件事，我以前从来没住过院，您能不能帮我给医院打个电话，联系住院的事，或者让他们帮我安排一个翻译？Alright. I know that if you have broken bones it is a big hassle and will take a long time to heal. I would like to ask you to do me another favour. I’ve never been admitted to hospital before so could you please call the hospital to make the necessary arrangements for me or ask them to hire an interpreter for me?
E6No worries Mrs. Li. I’ll send the results back to your family doctor and either your GP or I will ring the hospital for you. I believe the hospital has an interpreting service available.35
M6多谢您了，大夫。Thank you, doctor.

entry.title: 工伤复查
entry.content:
A doctor is interviewing a patient, Mr. Xue, about an injury he received as a result of a work accident.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Xue. Thanks for coming. I will ask you some questions about the accident and then we will examine your eyes. How is your right eye?28
M1感觉涩涩的。不舒服。您刚才一开灯我的右眼就感觉到疼痛。我现在对强光特别敏感。It’s dry and it feels uncomfortable. My right eye started to hurt as soon as you turned on the light. I am now especially sensitive to bright light.
E2I understand. From the file I can see that you were a gyprocker and you got injured on the 18th of March this year while you were working. Is that right?31
M2对。我当时正在一块金属板上钻洞突然间就感觉到一个很尖的东西飞进了眼睛里。可能是金属碎屑。我疼痛难忍我的同事马上送我去了医院。Yes. I was drilling holes on a metal sheet and suddenly something very sharp got into my eye. It could have been a piece of metal. The pain was killing me and my co-workers immediately took me to the hospital.
E3According to the file here, you stayed in the hospital for about two weeks. You had two operations after the injury. Have you gone back to the hospital or the specialist since you were discharged from the hospital? Are you on any medication now?44
M3出院后我还没有回过医院也没有去看过眼科医生。但是医生要我九月份的时候回去进行复查。I haven’t gone back to the hospital or seen an ophthalmologist after being discharged. But the doctor asked me to see him again in September.
E4I see. I will have a look at your right eye in a second. Before we do so, can you please tell me all your concerns about your right eye and how it affects your daily life?37
M4如果看到强光，眼睛就会疼，最主要的问题就是我看东西都是重影的，看东西都很模糊。眼睛有问题对我的生活有很大的影响。It’s painful whenever I see bright light and the main problem is I have double and blurred vision. This eye problem has caused me a lot of trouble in my daily life.
E5Can you give some examples, like what things you were able to do but can no longer do since the injury?19
M5我现在已经不能再回到原来的地方工作了，而且也不能开车，害怕出危险。两只眼睛经常涩涩的，还经常发红。I can’t go back to my previous job anymore and I’m not able to drive anymore because I am afraid of having a car accident. My eyes are often dry and they often turn red.
E6Thanks Mr. Xue. Please follow me to another room and I’ll examine your eyes. We should be finished very soon.20
M6好的，谢谢你，医生。Alright. Thanks Doctor.

entry.title: 戒酒失败
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Zhong, a recovering alcoholic, and Mr. James, a social worker, about the progress of Mr. Zhong’s recovery.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, Mr. Zhong. Thanks for coming in today. I’d like to have a chat with you and see how you are doing these days.
M1你好，James 先生。老实说，我最近又开始喝酒了，我妻子因此很伤心，她不想看到我做出了这么大的努力最后还是失败。Hello, Mr James. To be honest, I’ve started drinking again recently. My wife is very sad about it. She doesn’t want to see me fail after all the effort I’ve put in.
E2I see. A little setback is inevitable. In the initial stages, you should set a more realistic goal, say, not drinking for at least three days per week.
M2我也知道要一步一步来，但我越不让自己喝越是想喝。而且我清醒的时候感觉很空虚害怕， 醉了反而舒服。I know I need to take one step at a time, but the harder I try to stop myself from drinking, the stronger my cravings become. Also, I feel empty and afraid when I’m sober, but comfortable when I’m drunk.
E3Those are called withdrawal symptoms. It’s because you are dependent on alcohol, both physically and psychologically. To reduce your dependence, you need to stay away from alcohol and your old drinking buddies as much as possible.
M3这点我深有体会。几个老朋友上星期他们硬要拉我去喝酒，结果我们喝了个通宵。我不能一直这样下去。I can totally relate to that. Several old friends insisted on having a drink with me last week, and we ended up drinking the whole night. I can’t keep doing this.
E4I’m glad you’ve realised the importance of a positive environment for your recovery. That’s why you need a strong support group that helps and encourages you throughout the process.29
M4我知道。可是我内心深处还是很不安，我的生活一团糟，看不到希望，我总觉得自己是个受害者。I understand. But I still feel uneasy deep down. My life is a mess and I can’t see any hope. I often feel like I’m a victim.
E5Well, self-pity is likely to lead you back into drinking again. Why not see things in a different way? You are taking steps to regain control of your life. That’s very brave.32
M5对呀，我应该乐观一点的。人们常说，态度决定一切。我要试着改变自己的态度。That’s right. I should be more optimistic. People often say that attitude is everything. I’ll try to change my attitude.
E6That’s the spirit! Remember you are not in this alone. Give us a call or come back anytime when you need support.22
M6太谢谢了。我会尽自己的努力让生活重回正轨的。Thank you so much. I’ll do my best to get my life back on track.

entry.title: 裁员社保
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between James (J), a Centrelink Officer, and Mr Liang (L). Mr Liang is enquiring about his eligibility for a Low Income Health Care Card.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1不好意思啊，先生！我迟到了几分钟。是巴士晚点了，真的很抱歉。I am very sorry sir. I’m a few minutes late. The bus was late, I am really sorry.
M1No worries Mr. Liang. My name is James Morris. How may I help you today?15
E2最近我们公司需要缩减开支，必须要裁员，我也是其中之一。所以我来这看看有什么福利我可以申请。Recently, my employer needed to reduce his expenses and had to make some staff redundant, and I was made redundant. That’s why I’m here to see what sorts of benefits I can apply for.
M2You may be eligible for the “New Start Allowance” and a “Low Income Health Care Card”. Are you still engaged in any sort of employment, for example, working part-time or working as a casual employee?35
E3有呀。我仍然在我朋友的餐馆做服务员。每周工作三天。虽然薪水不算好，不过，好就好在它可以包午餐和晚餐。Yes, I’m working in my friend’s restaurant as a waiter. I work 3 days a week. The pay is not that good but the good thing is that lunch and dinner are provided.
M3In this case, I’m afraid that you can only apply for the latter one, which is the “Low Income Health Care Card”. It gives low-income earners access to cheaper prescription medicines, and various concessions from government and private organisations.40
E4听起来不错。那我该怎么申请这张卡呢？我身体不大好，所以总是要去看医生、买药什么的。如果有了这张卡，一定会帮到我很多的。It sounds great. How can I apply for this card? I have some health issues so I always need to see a doctor and buy medication. It will help me a lot if I have this card.
M4You may be eligible for the card if you meet the income test. For a single person, your weekly income must not exceed $497 or $3,976 in 8 weeks.39
E5我的收入肯定没有这个数！我还有个问题，这个卡是跟政府的保健医疗卡一样，有五年的有效期吗？My income is definitely less than that. I have one more question, is this card valid for 5 years, just like the Medicare card?
M5Mr Liang, you need to renew this card every 6 months and every time you are required to show evidence in order to qualify for it.26
E6什么，这么麻烦？那我都需要带什么材料呢？我能用我的工资单和银行对账单作为证明材料吗？你这儿有申请表吗？What? That’s a real hassle! So what should I bring? Can I use my payslips and bank statements as evidence? Do you have the application form here?
M6Yes, that’s fine. I can give you a copy of the application form, just a second.16

entry.title: 社福拒保
entry.content:
This dialogue is between a Centrelink officer and Mr. Jiang, whose application for a Centrelink payment has just been refused.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Morning，Mr Jiang. We are here to discuss the benefit that you applied for. I am afraid the payment you applied for cannot be approved.
M1为什么啊？我现在经济很拮据，所以我很需要这笔家庭补助，我的条件不是都符合吗？为什么拿不到补助呢？Why? I am now experiencing financial hardship so I really need this payment. Don’t I meet all the requirements? Why can’t I get it?
E2Mr. Jiang, as the guidelines indicate, in order to be granted the benefit, you are subject to both an income test and an assets test. You cannot be eligible for the benefit unless you pass both of them.
M2我现在失业，我妻子自从生完孩子会就因为身体原因无法上班，我们现在没有其他经济来源，连房子都是租的，这还不够条件吗？I don’t have a job now and my wife can no longer go to work because of her physical condition after giving birth. We don’t have any other source of income and we live in a rented place. Isn’t that enough for us to be eligible?
E3Mr. Jiang, I understand that you are in financial difficulty, which is in line with the result of our investigation. Basically, you have passed the income test but problems occurred when it came to the assets test.
M3资产测试不就是检查我们名下的资产状况吗？我们现在基本没什么资产，银行就剩那点存款了，你们完全可以去查。另外，我们也没有任何房产，现在的房子都是租的，我也提供了租赁合同不是吗？The asset test is to assess the assets under our names, right? We don’t have many assets except for our small savings in the bank. You can go and check it. Apart from that, we don’t have any properties and we’re renting the place we live in. I have provided the lease, haven’t I?
E4Mr. Jiang, our officers have investigated your assets thoroughly and it was found out that you did not report your assets truthfully. Your savings and lease are fine. However, it seems that you did not provide a proper account in terms of property possession.
M4我的资产都在这了，除了房子，我们也没有任何的股票证券之类的投资，总之我们就是一穷二白啊，我们应该享受社会福利啊。My assets are all here. Except the house, we don’t have any other investment like stocks and securities. We are very poor and I think we are entitled to the social welfare benefits.
E5Even though you do not have property yourself, you may be able to inherit a house from one of your distant relatives with no heirs or family, which you didn’t inform us about. In this case, it can be considered as intentionally providing misleading or false information for financial benefit.
M5我不是故意的啊，的确是有这么回事，但谁知道以后会怎么样啊，我当时就想，万一写上这一点，会影响我的测试结果，所以就没写。I didn’t mean to do so. That’s true but no one knows what will happen later. I thought if I put it in my application, it would affect the test results, so I didn’t.
E6Mr. Jiang, you are obliged to provide truthful information to Centrelink. Otherwise, you are committing an offence and you can make us doubt the overall credibility of your documents, thus severely affecting your eligibility for the benefit.
M6我知道了，我真的不是故意的，我发誓，以后一定如实报告一切情况，绝不遗漏。I see. I didn’t do it on purpose, I swear. I will report everything truthfully next time with no information missing.

entry.title: 丧妻社福
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Hu and Larry, a Centrelink customer service officer. Mr. Hu has recently lost his wife.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Hu. Please come in and have a seat. Now, how can I help you today?17
M1我的妻子最近因为乳腺癌去世了。我的朋友建议我来这里咨询一下，看看能否获得什么帮助。My wife passed away of breast cancer recently. My friend recommended that I come here and make an enquiry about what kind of help I can get from you.
E2I’m very sorry for your loss, Mr. Hu. Are you receiving any income support payments at the moment? If not, you may be entitled to a Bereavement Allowance.29
M2没有，我一直都在工作，开大巴，虽然工资不高，但还比较稳定。我妻子在世的时候有领疾病津贴，因为她身患癌症不能工作。No. I have been working the whole time as a coach driver. The pay is not high but it’s stable. My wife was receiving a Sickness Allowance before she died because she was diagnosed with cancer and was unable to work.
E3In that case, you should be eligible for the allowance but the amount you can get depends on your assets and income. I’m sure you have gone through these kinds of tests before.33
M3是的，但我只知道你们需要评估我的资产。但我还是有些疑问。比如，哪些资产是需要被评估的？我有一处投资的房产，这要算在内吗？Yes, but I only knew that you would assess my assets. I still have some questions. For example, which assets should be included in this assessment? I have an investment property. Does it need to be included?
E4Yes, we need to assess the value of your assets, which includes any money you have, any real estate you own other than your principle home and any motor vehicles you own. You can find the complete list on this fact sheet.42
M4那收入测试是怎样的呢？我现在一周大概能挣个六百来块钱。我在银行还有五万元的定期存款，每年能有大概三千元的利息。您觉得我满足收入测试的要求吗？How does the income test work? I earn about 600 dollars a week now and I have a 50,000-dollar term deposit in the bank and it yields about 3,000 dollars annually. Do I meet the requirements of the income test?
E5Well, I can’t give you a definite answer right now before the test is done. However, if your fortnightly income is less than $1647.60 you may be eligible for a part pension.32
M5其实钱有多少我无所谓。最大的痛苦是以后的人生我只能一个人度过了。我不知道我能否忍受这样的孤独。我要是跟我妻子一起去另一个世界就好了。In fact, it doesn’t matter how much I can get. The most painful thing is that I have to live alone for the rest of my life. I don’t know if I can stand such loneliness. I wish I could have gone with my wife.
E6You really have my sympathy Mr. Hu. I understand this is a really tough time for you. We can refer you to an expert grievance counsellor or other such services. I’m sure they can help you get through all this.40
M6好的，非常感谢你的热心帮助。等我准备好材料再来见你。Ok. Thank you for your kind help. I will come back to see you when I have the documents ready.

entry.title: 公司福利
entry.content:
The dialogue between Mrs. Li and Hector resumes after Hector finishes talking with his supervisor.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Sorry for keeping you waiting Mrs. Li. Let’s get back to business. We were talking about…16
M1你说我也许还能获得其他的福利，比如药品，房租什么的。You said I may be eligible for other welfare benefits as well, such as something for medicine or rent.
E2Right. Normally Sickness Allowance recipients are entitled to other payments. First is the Pharmaceutical Allowance, which helps you with the cost of prescription medicines. The fortnightly payment is $3.10 for each person.32
M2这也太少了吧，还不够我买盒止痛药呢。不管了，聊胜于无吧。那你说的租房补助呢？That’s not even enough for me to buy a pack of painkillers. Never mind, it’s better than nothing. What about rent assistance?
E3Given you are over 25, with no dependent children and are going to receive the Sickness Allowance, you can receive rent assistance as long as you are not renting public accommodation.33
M3这太好了，我是和私人房东签的租约，房租每周 400 块。你说我现在没有孩子，所以可以领租房补贴，那我生了孩子之后是不是就领不了了？That’s great. I signed the lease with a private landlord and the rent is 400 dollars per week. You said that I am eligible for rent assistance because I don’t have any kids. Will I still be able to receive it after I give birth to my child?
E4That’s not the case. Normally, if you have a dependent child and get more than the base rate of the Family Tax Benefit, you can still keep receiving the money. So don’t worry too much, Mrs Li.37
M4是这样啊。我记得你说过我还可以获得电话费补助的。我家没有固话，只有手机，这包含在内吗？Oh, I see. I remember you also mentioned that I can get a telephone allowance. But I don’t have a fixed line at home, I only have a mobile phone. Does that count?
E5Let me check. Oh, sorry, I’m afraid you are not entitled to the allowance because only Sickness Allowance recipients who are over 60 can receive a telephone allowance.28
M5哦，没关系。虽然每笔福利的数额并不大，但加在一起还是挺可观的。确实能解我们的燃眉之急。Well, no worries. Each payment is not much but they’ll add up to a sizeable sum of money. It will definitely help solve our immediate problems.
E6Now, I’m going to get all the forms you need. Just fill them out and bring them back with all the supporting documents to make a claim.27
M6非常感谢你的耐心帮助。等我丈夫伤好了，我会让他来当面感谢你的。Thank you for your patience and help. I will ask my husband to come and thank you in person when he recovers from his injuries.
